title: (No AI) AI ‘content creators’ are getting harder to spot
author: Robert Hart
content:
![Aitana Lopez, AI avatar by creative agency The Clueless.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/GAUYdq3a4AAAlsX.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Aitana Lopez, AI avatar by creative agency The Clueless. | Image: The Clueless

_This is_ [The Stepback](https://www.theverge.com/the-stepback-newsletter), _a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on AI confusion, [follow Robert Hart](https://www.theverge.com/authors/robert-hart)._ The Stepback _arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for_ The Stepback _[here](https://www.theverge.com/newsletters)._

## How it started

At first, AI influencers were relatively easy to identify - and to ignore. Aside from the occasional bursts of hype, they didn't seem to change much about the way social media worked. The earliest virtual influencers - [Lil Miquela](https://www.instagram.com/lilmiquela/?hl=en) with her blunt fringe and freckles, [Imma](https://www.instagram.com/imma.gram/?hl=en) with her bubblegum pink bob, and [Shudu Gram](https://www.instagram.com/shudu.gram/?hl=en) with her flawless complexion - were obviously digital productions. [Collaborations](https://www.theverge.com/2020/8/31/21408626/ikea-tokyo-imma-virtual-influencer) …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/943187/ai-content-creators)

title: (No AI) The mayor of Shelbyville, Indiana, says only people who live in ‘shitty houses’ oppose data center
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Photo collage of a data center.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/STKS528_DATA_CENTERS_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A proposed [$2 billion data center](https://www.therepublic.com/2026/04/10/shelbyville-common-council-advances-plans-for-2b-data-center-complex/) has become a political flashpoint in the small city of Shelbyville, Indiana. And the controversy has only grown more intense after the mayor, Scott Furgeson, was [caught on camera](https://www.tiktok.com/@moreperfectunion/video/7647663715872967950?_r=1&_t=ZT-96yS8CT6EmL) saying of the "No Data Center" signs going up that, "I've seen a lot of these all over town, but I only see them in shitty houses," before adding, "most of them are rentals."

The woman speaking to him in the clip quickly pushes back, saying that they're "working class," and someone chimes in to add something that a mayor shouldn't have to be told about their constituents: "it doesn't matter whether they're rentals, they're still hu …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/944984/shelbyville-indiana-mayor-shitty-houses-data-center)

The woman speaking to him in the clip quickly pushes back, saying that they're "working class," and someone chimes in to add something that a mayor shouldn't have to be told about their constituents: "it doesn't matter whether they're rentals, they're still hu …

creator: Steven Levy
title: (No AI) Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?
dc:creator: Steven Levy
content: Microsoft’s AI products aren’t selling, and Github’s been plagued with troubles. WIRED spoke with VP Scott Hanselman about whether the company is in catch-up mode.
contentSnippet: Microsoft’s AI products aren’t selling, and Github’s been plagued with troubles. WIRED spoke with VP Scott Hanselman about whether the company is in catch-up mode.
